title: dalbergia ovata var. ovata
intro: Dalbergia ovata var. ovata, also known as the Indian rosewood or East Indian rosewood, is a tree species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Dalbergia ovata var. ovata include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating gastrointestinal problems, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and cardio-protective effects. In herbal preparations, the bark of the tree is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and decoctions, and can also be used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments.
